metadata.validation: SRS-aware with placeholder blocking and deduplication

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Download mobile application from App Store
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that a user can successfully download the mobile application from the App Store.
preconditions: User has an Apple device with iOS version 14 or higher
test_steps: Step 1: Open the App Store on the mobile device and search for 'Restaurant/Food Service' app
Step 2: Select the app icon from the search results and click 'Get'
Step 3: Authenticate with Apple ID if prompted, then wait for the download to complete
Step 4: Verify that the app is installed on the device by checking the home screen
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application is successfully downloaded and installed on the device.

phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that an error is thrown when attempting to download the mobile application from the App Store with invalid inputs.
preconditions: User has an Apple device with iOS version 14 or higher
test_steps: Step 1: Open the App Store on the mobile device and search for 'InvalidApp' (non-existent app)
Step 2: Select the non-existent app icon from the search results and click 'Get'
Step 3: Verify that an error message is displayed indicating that the app does not exist
Step 4: Confirm that the download process is cancelled
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed indicating that the app does not exist, and the download process is cancelled.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: Maximum results for App Store search
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that the maximum number of results is displayed when searching for the mobile application from the App Store.
preconditions: User has an Apple device with iOS version 14 or higher
test_steps: Step 1: Open the App Store on the mobile device and search for 'Restaurant/Food Service' app
Step 2: Verify that the search results display a maximum of 100 apps
Step 3: Confirm that no more than 100 apps are displayed in the search results
Step 4: Verify that scrolling through the search results does not reveal additional apps
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The App Store search results display a maximum of 100 apps, and no more than 100 apps are displayed.

phase1_test_cases.test_title: App Store download integration with other features
phase1_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile application can be successfully downloaded from the App Store and integrated with other features.
preconditions: User has an Apple device with iOS version 14 or higher
test_steps: Step 1: Open the App Store on the mobile device and search for 'Restaurant/Food Service' app
Step 2: Select the app icon from the search results and click 'Get'
Step 3: Verify that the app is installed on the device by checking the home screen
Step 4: Launch the app and verify that it functions as expected
phase1_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application can be successfully downloaded from the App Store, installed, and launched without errors.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Mobile App Download Performance Test: Initial Load
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile app downloads and initializes within a reasonable time frame.
preconditions: Device has sufficient storage space
Internet connection available
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the mobile app on a device with a stable internet connection
Step 2: Measure the time it takes for the app to download and initialize (approx. 10 seconds)
Step 3: Verify that the app's UI is fully loaded and interactive within 15 seconds
Step 4: Check that the app's core features are functional, such as menu navigation and ordering
Step 5: Measure the time it takes for the app to render a list of available restaurants (approx. 2-3 seconds)
Step 6: Verify that the list is scrollable and displays at least 10 restaurant options
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile app downloads and initializes within 15 seconds, with a maximum of 2-3 seconds to render the list of available restaurants.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Mobile App Download Performance Test: Subsequent Loads
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile app loads subsequent times within a reasonable time frame.
preconditions: Device has sufficient storage space
Internet connection available
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the mobile app on a device with a stable internet connection
Step 2: Measure the time it takes for the app to load subsequent times (approx. 5 seconds)
Step 3: Verify that the app's UI is fully loaded and interactive within 10 seconds
Step 4: Check that the app's core features are functional, such as menu navigation and ordering
Step 5: Measure the time it takes for the app to render a list of available restaurants (approx. 1-2 seconds)
Step 6: Verify that the list is scrollable and displays at least 10 restaurant options
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile app loads subsequent times within 10 seconds, with a maximum of 1-2 seconds to render the list of available restaurants.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Mobile App Download Performance Test: Large Data Load
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile app can handle large amounts of data within a reasonable time frame.
preconditions: Device has sufficient storage space
Internet connection available
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the mobile app on a device with a stable internet connection
Step 2: Measure the time it takes for the app to load a large list of restaurants (approx. 10-15 seconds)
Step 3: Verify that the app's UI is fully loaded and interactive within 20 seconds
Step 4: Check that the app can handle scrolling through the list without significant performance degradation
Step 5: Measure the time it takes for the app to load a large list of menu items (approx. 10-15 seconds)
Step 6: Verify that the app's UI remains responsive and interactive during data loading
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile app can handle large amounts of data within 20 seconds, with a maximum of 10-15 seconds to load the list of restaurants and menu items.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Unauthorized access to downloaded app
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that an unauthorized user cannot access or modify the downloaded mobile application
preconditions: The mobile application is downloaded and installed on a device
test_steps: Step 1: Log in as an unauthorized user with invalid credentials (username: 'invalid', password: 'wrong')
Step 2: Attempt to access the downloaded app without logging in
Step 3: Verify that the app is not accessible or displays an error message
Step 4: Attempt to modify the app's settings or data without logging in
Step 5: Verify that any attempted modifications are rejected or rolled back
Step 6: Confirm that the unauthorized user cannot access or modify the downloaded app
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The unauthorized user is unable to access or modify the downloaded mobile application, and any attempted modifications are rejected or rolled back.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Data leakage during app download
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that sensitive data is not leaked during the mobile application download process
preconditions: The mobile application is being downloaded
test_steps: Step 1: Monitor network traffic during the app download process using a packet sniffer (e.g., Wireshark)
Step 2: Verify that no sensitive data (e.g., user credentials, payment information) is transmitted or stored
Step 3: Check for any potential vulnerabilities in the download process (e.g., unencrypted data transmission)
Step 4: Confirm that the app's download process does not compromise sensitive data
Step 5: Verify that the downloaded app does not store or transmit sensitive data without explicit user consent
Step 6: Confirm that any stored data is properly encrypted and secured
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application download process does not leak sensitive data, and all transmitted or stored data is properly encrypted and secured.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: App update vulnerability
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile application's update mechanism does not introduce vulnerabilities or compromise sensitive data
preconditions: The mobile application is installed and updated
test_steps: Step 1: Monitor network traffic during the app update process using a packet sniffer (e.g., Wireshark)
Step 2: Verify that no sensitive data (e.g., user credentials, payment information) is transmitted or stored
Step 3: Check for any potential vulnerabilities in the update mechanism (e.g., unencrypted data transmission)
Step 4: Confirm that the app's update process does not compromise sensitive data
Step 5: Verify that the updated app does not store or transmit sensitive data without explicit user consent
Step 6: Confirm that any stored data is properly encrypted and secured
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application's update mechanism does not introduce vulnerabilities or compromise sensitive data, and all transmitted or stored data is properly encrypted and secured.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Downloading Mobile App: Initial Experience
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the user can successfully download and install the mobile app, with a smooth initial experience.
preconditions: Mobile device with internet connection
App store or Google Play Store
test_steps: Step 1: Open the app store (Apple App Store or Google Play Store) on the mobile device
Step 2: Search for the restaurant/food service mobile app and select it from search results
Step 3: Tap the 'Get' or 'Install' button to initiate the download process
Step 4: Wait for the download to complete (expected time: < 30 seconds)
Step 5: Verify that the app icon appears on the mobile device's home screen
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The user successfully downloads and installs the mobile app within 30 seconds, with no errors or issues.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Downloading Mobile App: Error Handling
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the user can handle errors during the download process, such as network connectivity issues.
preconditions: Mobile device with internet connection
App store or Google Play Store
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate a network connectivity issue (e.g., turn off Wi-Fi)
Step 2: Initiate the download process by tapping the 'Get' or 'Install' button
Step 3: Verify that an error message appears, indicating the connection issue
Step 4: Wait for the user to resolve the connectivity issue (e.g., turn on Wi-Fi)
Step 5: Re-initiate the download process and verify successful completion
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The user can successfully handle errors during the download process, with clear error messages and minimal disruption.

test_data.actual_error_message: Error: Unable to connect to the internet. Please check your connection and try again.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Downloading Mobile App: Navigation
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the user can navigate to the app's main screen after installation, with intuitive navigation.
preconditions: Mobile device with installed mobile app
test_steps: Step 1: Launch the mobile app on the mobile device
Step 2: Verify that the app's main screen appears, with clear navigation options (e.g., menu icon)
Step 3: Tap the menu icon to access additional features or settings
Step 4: Verify that the user can navigate back to the main screen using the 'Back' button or other navigation methods
Step 5: Verify that the app's main screen remains accessible and functional
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The user can successfully navigate to the app's main screen after installation, with intuitive navigation options and minimal cognitive load.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: iOS vs Android Download Comparison
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the download process is consistent across iOS and Android platforms.
preconditions: Device with iOS or Android operating system
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application on an iOS device (e.g., iPhone 12) and initiate the download process
Step 2: Verify that the download progress bar appears correctly and updates in real-time
Step 3: Simultaneously, open the same mobile application on an Android device (e.g., Samsung Galaxy S22) and initiate the download process
Step 4: Compare the download progress bars on both devices to ensure they update consistently and accurately
Step 5: Verify that the downloaded file is successfully installed on both devices
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The download process completes successfully on both iOS and Android devices, with identical progress bar updates and successful installation of the downloaded file.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Screen Size and Resolution Compatibility
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile application is compatible with different screen sizes and resolutions.
preconditions: Device with varying screen sizes and resolutions (e.g., iPhone 12, Samsung Galaxy S22, Google Pixel 4 XL)
test_steps: Step 1: Open the mobile application on an iPhone 12 with a 1080x1920 resolution
Step 2: Verify that all UI elements are correctly displayed and interactive
Step 3: Switch to a Samsung Galaxy S22 with a 1440x3040 resolution and repeat Step 1
Step 4: Verify that the mobile application adapts to the new screen size and resolution without issues
Step 5: Repeat Steps 1-4 on a Google Pixel 4 XL with a 1080x2160 resolution
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application is compatible with different screen sizes and resolutions, displaying correctly and interacting smoothly across all tested devices.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: GPS and Location Accuracy
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the mobile application accurately uses GPS and location services across different devices and networks.
preconditions: Device with GPS capabilities (e.g., iPhone 12, Samsung Galaxy S22)
test_steps: Step 1: Enable GPS on an iPhone 12 and initiate a location-based action in the mobile application
Step 2: Verify that the mobile application accurately uses GPS coordinates and displays correct location information
Step 3: Switch to a Samsung Galaxy S22 and repeat Step 1
Step 4: Verify that the mobile application adapts to the new device's GPS capabilities without issues
Step 5: Test the mobile application on different network types (e.g., WiFi, 4G, 5G) to ensure accurate location services
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mobile application accurately uses GPS and location services across different devices and networks, displaying correct location information and adapting to varying network conditions.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: API Test: Download Mobile Application
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the API returns a successful response when downloading the mobile application.
preconditions: The user has a valid account and is logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/mobile-app with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 2: Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
Step 3: Verify response body contains the mobile application download link
Step 4: Verify that the downloaded file is a valid APK file
Step 5: Verify that the downloaded file size is within the expected range (less than or equal to 10MB)
Step 6: Verify that the downloaded file can be installed on the device
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns a successful response with HTTP 200 status code, and the downloaded file is a valid APK file within the expected size range.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: API Test: Download Mobile Application (Error Handling)
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the API returns an error response when downloading the mobile application fails.
preconditions: The user has a valid account and is logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/mobile-app with header Authorization: Bearer <invalid_jwt_token>
Step 2: Verify HTTP 401 status code returned
Step 3: Verify response body contains an error message indicating invalid token
Step 4: Repeat Step 1 with a different invalid token
Step 5: Verify that the API returns the same error response (HTTP 401) and error message
Step 6: Verify that the API does not return any sensitive information
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API returns an error response with HTTP 401 status code and a clear error message when downloading the mobile application fails.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: API Test: Download Mobile Application (Rate Limiting)
phase2_test_cases.description: Verifies that the API enforces rate limiting when downloading the mobile application.
preconditions: The user has a valid account and is logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Send GET request to /api/mobile-app with header Authorization: Bearer <valid_jwt_token>
Step 2: Verify HTTP 200 status code returned
Step 3: Repeat Step 1 multiple times (5-10 requests) within a short time frame (less than or equal to 1 second)
Step 4: Verify that the API returns an error response with HTTP 429 status code and a rate limiting message
Step 5: Wait for a longer time period (more than 30 seconds) before repeating Step 3
Step 6: Verify that the API allows the download request again after the rate limiting period has passed
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The API enforces rate limiting and returns an error response with HTTP 429 status code when downloading the mobile application too frequently.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Download Mobile App: CRUD Operations Validation
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate CRUD operations (Create, Read, Update, Delete) for restaurant menu items
preconditions: Mobile app is installed and logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Create a new menu item with name 'Test Item' and price $10.99
Step 2: Verify the newly created menu item appears in the list view
Step 3: Update the price of the test item to $11.99
Step 4: Verify the updated price is reflected in the list view
Step 5: Delete the test item from the list view
Step 6: Verify the test item no longer appears in the list view
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The CRUD operations (Create, Read, Update, Delete) for menu items are validated and reflected correctly in the mobile app.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Download Mobile App: Mandatory vs Optional Field Validation
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the mandatory and optional fields for restaurant menu items
preconditions: Mobile app is installed and logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Attempt to create a new menu item without name
Step 2: Verify an error message appears indicating the 'Name' field is required
Step 3: Create a new menu item with only the 'Name' field filled out
Step 4: Verify the 'Price' field is not mandatory and can be left blank
Step 5: Attempt to update the price of an existing menu item without providing a value
Step 6: Verify the 'Price' field remains unchanged
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The mandatory and optional fields for restaurant menu items are validated correctly, with required fields preventing creation or update of incomplete data.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Download Mobile App: Foreign Key Relationships
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate the foreign key relationships between restaurant menu items and categories
preconditions: Mobile app is installed and logged in
test_steps: Step 1: Create a new category 'Test Category'
Step 2: Create a new menu item 'Test Item' under the newly created category
Step 3: Verify the menu item appears in the list view for its respective category
Step 4: Attempt to move the menu item to a different category
Step 5: Verify an error message appears indicating the menu item cannot be moved due to foreign key constraints
Step 6: Verify the menu item remains in its original category
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The foreign key relationships between restaurant menu items and categories are validated correctly, preventing invalid changes to menu item categorization.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Network Interruption Handling: App Crash Recovery
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the app recovers from a network interruption and crashes without losing user data.
preconditions: App is installed on a device with stable internet connection
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate network interruption by disabling Wi-Fi/Cellular connection for 10 seconds
Step 2: Trigger app crash by navigating to a non-existent page or performing an invalid action
Step 3: Wait for 30 seconds to ensure the app has crashed and user data is persisted
Step 4: Re-enable network connection and restart the app
Step 5: Verify that user data is still present and not lost after crash recovery
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app recovers from a network interruption and crashes without losing user data, with all previous actions preserved.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: GPS Signal Loss Behavior: App Crash Recovery
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the app recovers from a GPS signal loss and crashes without losing user location data.
preconditions: App is installed on a device with GPS enabled
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate GPS signal loss by disabling GPS for 10 seconds
Step 2: Trigger app crash by navigating to a non-existent page or performing an invalid action
Step 3: Wait for 30 seconds to ensure the app has crashed and user location data is persisted
Step 4: Re-enable GPS signal and restart the app
Step 5: Verify that user location data is still present and not lost after crash recovery
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app recovers from a GPS signal loss and crashes without losing user location data, with all previous actions preserved.

phase2_test_cases.test_title: Session Timeout Handling: Data Persistence Across Crashes
phase2_test_cases.description: Validate that the app persists user data across crashes and session timeouts without losing previous actions.
preconditions: App is installed on a device with stable internet connection
test_steps: Step 1: Simulate session timeout by keeping the app open for 10 minutes
Step 2: Trigger app crash by navigating to a non-existent page or performing an invalid action
Step 3: Wait for 30 seconds to ensure the app has crashed and user data is persisted
Step 4: Re-start the app and verify that previous actions are still present
Step 5: Verify that user data is not lost across crashes and session timeouts
phase2_test_cases.expected_result: The app persists user data across crashes and session timeouts without losing previous actions, with all data preserved.
